subject: Food Order Signed, Iran’s 'Robots,' and 99 Christmas Dessert Recipes
body:
President Trump signed an [order launching new federal task forces](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGypCAQxvHTaMYUMjAMAcEmk71NN7QQGu1dBAtafXP7LecKL-_6V_--P1_jl8OEef7tVrAUy5jhbAmIoI6xlFBqgNpwzhCwutxqmQqNggvFBsH4cwwO03uUfbARQMC9BzvohxrMYMy9X6zU3kxSP7WIQRmlBNeTCYZH48FLo3q0V20Q_DkM_HHnt0dUU5Tca6f8pJXsJIeAVOrtb8FMC8T0LgfUmy9rn-xCtLXu_qsTr068zvO8xfI97Q0ztHbddOK1lYSEvnXiRXVfN3aJGoNv8DvhAezDZL6CI8wzu-Ss7duW3swvDjMj1_6xWKqHxlwIFVpjLhMyX9YNCD-VCRZ3YKl9tWE7MSVsD91JPq8O0-dd-tHGhxX_AwAA__9h1ZZd) to crack down on suspected price fixing and anti-competitive practices in the food industry.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkcuq2zAQhp9G3sVII18XXpQYr3q6aUvpSsjSyFGrS5AnCXn74jaFQpdndvPz8_Ex8-1NvWkffNo-6YgTuawSPvaARFiUy9nmYrHsfktofdFpL3nNpIBDexJw4oOy2oenaio7OURAWeEk-q4VoxhHWV0mIZCb9hgwY9Mib8dxGHrowa26F13lp4MmgA9C8E7yunPt6hpuet2atW8b1nC0nnKpf2Sf6IIuPPMdS21yrMJ0IbruTH5gsDBYNn_H2pRbncvGYPmDPj10iWvQ6SfSXl8oBiaXs45X7bd0zhaZnL8s5-XjdwZdzFYHJueDdNq8IwadZXIGGHkPkkFnju1_MIPuRlHt-VbMQXxpvuKI1t8ik7P1mycdXrF5WfxrcOSE5SjLdhAg_nZzIkzE5HzNOymfzFN9_axcDqoqk70-fAh-73rW8C1qH34fiN711fsEvwIAAP__PAW0OA) | |
# Monday, December 8, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1980, The Beatles' superstar [John Lennon](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOrCAUxvGnwQ4DKKgFxW3s5rZbGpSjnAlyJkDG-Pab2ewbbP1Lvi_5fz2Wh8OI6fjvTrB1pyXBVSLUCnnZiTxlD7ngkcBjdqlkWqkuSijNpeJiXLzDeC994-0OoKBrwMrBaDnJaeqaYMGNRuteyMEbIzcp_To5A16NqpOdGxu0nzWpxCilMJ1oza7XvRfb4PS2DrpnvQCPlXL7JEw1wB5vekNuNzqbaEOtr8K6f0zNTM3XdbUBS6V8f5ypuQYs3LubY-K_wtTsYYNzhcxHpuYnhcQjpESJl0C1yda_LowRixlYL47TYfy5q39q9LbqOwAA__9383we) was shot and killed by Mark David Chapman as he was entering his Manhattan apartment building, generating worldwide mourning. The tallest Christmas tree in one English town isn’t covered in tinsel or ornaments—it’s made [of 100,000 balloons](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2u3CAQxfGnwR0rGH_hgiIN3U2b0sJmWJOMPSsY3ZXfPtq8Quoj_XT0__W1fsVC5Xr-jCd6ybxe-G6EIljXzJy4JqytPC9MpcarVd5YVjAwagvauDXFQvc6dMlnRMC-Q2_nabSLXZa-O7yDxbm0zABmRJf2oXc47ejSEDeIbuqK_2gWjLPWTL15THnc8mD2OY77No-DGgymIlwfv7lccmCmm7-xPnY-O_KHyKup_oeCoCBc94ubfCYF4QMrCBYUBDMpCFQyNrkJFYSDBUmn0l4U76Zh1JlZtEQivR-1NDlj01IR9RkTas7aGvNHb5GI-WoKQld9er0LUWnTrAbzPGOhf7fkv1p-e_gbAAD__02lh68). The 25-foot display, assembled by 20 volunteers over five days, is now drawing crowds and raising money for charity. What's the most unusual Christmas tree you've ever seen? Reply to this email to let us know. Mistake tracking: [Yesterday's Trivia](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1u8yAUxvGrwZsjOOYjHhjexVvetSMCc0hOhSECmih3X6Vrx-6Pnp_0_7i4i6dM5frfH2hHqq7gs2ccA5tLtcbaIrZO14KRmi-91VCHAw5qFjDzs4ue8svJKdqECLhMaIXRSqxiXZfpZrUMWkvvIUQMAnQKygjpcTdaIep1Ivt-E8DPQnC98JNOKiTJd-PVHoySTHKMNGo7fVYq44Ypv-oD22mvx5TtbYx7Z8s_BhuD7feEwTYaPcgz2PpXif41R9zxCNhmM79tBtvUbLw_KWfq2jDJr4en_AOMP1V5WPgOAAD__zNRdsc) incorrectly stated that Kentucky became a state in 1891. In fact, Kentucky achieved statehood in 1792; the 1891 date refers to the adoption of a revised state constitution. We regret the error. If you want to give someone on the streets more than just warmth this Christmas, your support of today’s sponsor, [the inner-city ministry of Cru®](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykksmq2zAUhp9G3sVo8Ljwoo3xqrebtpSuhCwdOWo1GPk4IW9f3F4CIcu7_T_-gcP5-SbflPMuLl9VgAFtkhFumwdEyNKmZFI2kDe3RDAuq7jlNCeUnPL6xPiJdtIo5--yKsxgATiIAgbWNjXrWd-L4jL0opttVwsFXNe6A2NbYJVpKWOaNg0UbjjSGKcdY7QRtGxsPduK6lbVem7rilQUjMOUy9_JRbyA9fd0hVzqFAo_XBDXjYhPhE-ET4u7QqnzXqa8ED79jz7dVA6zV_EP4FZeMHgiprMKq3JLPCcDRIzfp_P05RfhjQorEZ9DMsoTMR55p8VZfBBDxMh5T1suHpo-tNeqB98xyC3tWR9N7_OfYADj9kDEaNziUPknqN-Xvq48KEI-jKLuGGfPvhQRIhIxrmlD6aK-yx_fpE1eFnkw681577amJRVdgnL-30HxQ19wHfjfAAAA___vecQR), can help turn a night of survival into a moment of real hope with a Homeless Care Kit.
|
---
## Federal Task Forces to Probe Food Pricing
President Trump signed an [order launching new federal task forces](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGypCAQxvHTaMYUMjAMAcEmk71NN7QQGu1dBAtafXP7LecKL-_6V_--P1_jl8OEef7tVrAUy5jhbAmIoI6xlFBqgNpwzhCwutxqmQqNggvFBsH4cwwO03uUfbARQMC9BzvohxrMYMy9X6zU3kxSP7WIQRmlBNeTCYZH48FLo3q0V20Q_DkM_HHnt0dUU5Tca6f8pJXsJIeAVOrtb8FMC8T0LgfUmy9rn-xCtLXu_qsTr068zvO8xfI97Q0ztHbddOK1lYSEvnXiRXVfN3aJGoNv8DvhAezDZL6CI8wzu-Ss7duW3swvDjMj1_6xWKqHxlwIFVpjLhMyX9YNCD-VCRZ3YKl9tWE7MSVsD91JPq8O0-dd-tHGhxX_AwAA__9h1ZZd) to crack down on suspected price fixing and anti-competitive practices in the food industry. The Justice Department and Federal Trade Commission will investigate whether big players, including foreign-owned companies, are [driving up grocery bills](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOpCAURp9GOwwgohYU29jNtluaK1z17iAYYMbM22-cfYS__3LynfPnMT-APIXtNxxoyhrngFf2WAqmeY3RxeQwZdoCOkoQcopLLLPksmNCMj7MDsh_ZlU7syJKbGs0otedGMU4tvVuwAo-tr2VA6BeBLd6sEorUODavgVZk7lpQvJBCK5b3ui1W1bFbQ-dXfpOVYqjoxJT8zdSKDuu_hPfmBobj9qbvZQzV-2vSk6VnK7ravLrPP3H7kBhSUDhHlZyglTIesyVnJQc1chKeh0nu-Uy-3qyElkB-_TIwHvc0LG7AfsPZF8iS5SfuU7GnRd5T1n3leLbAeS_h8qPKr6N_BcAAP__hryJcw) or threatening the security of the nation’s food supply. If they find wrongdoing, the agencies can bring cases and recommend new rules. Lawmakers will receive progress reports within six months and again at the one-year mark, setting up a comprehensive review of America’s food system. [Read the order here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2ynDAQBODTiEyUED_LBgROyJ5Th9QgjWBsoaGkWfDe3rW-wsu7uqu_X1_LF1CktP2EAycJvCS8S0QRzEtg9pw95kJbQk8ZUsm8sizW2F43Vptx8UDxvXSVnwKixbbCqXkMffNsns-22qfedxjMOlrbjoNZDfQwrtC5AVYYg3MVTZ-2xpqxaczQmnoI_Ro64x7Qu_XRd6oz6Ek417-ZkuwY4psvzLXjo4rTLnIW1f5QdlZ2vu-7vncS3PlVsN74UnY-MxbymIQganBCnIqy82dX2bmxys7gfcZSKG26oHtlkrfOVP4UHTIf-szkUAf6-wlA8hqSkHZ8nCgkdKFecYeLOGtKWnbUHzxdXucZ39rtQEnZucqTP2-KkcrwUJ3ZDqD4_4Z8y_6a7L8AAAD__1Z0nXo).
## Miami, Alabama Make College Football Playoff
The 12-team College Football Playoff bracket is set, with Indiana earning the No. 1 seed after a 13-0 campaign, followed by Ohio State, Georgia, and Texas Tech, all of which receive first-round byes. [See the full bracket](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1uhDAQQOHTmA40Hsxf4SIN3aZNiQwes05mGWRbi_b20eYKqZ_0Fe_rttxc5Hjsn-5BtgRZDroyUymUliDiJXlKOe4H-ZjckZOsUhYE7GqNNYyLd5Ffi6m8DURIbUVWD32nJz1NbXW3xmgATRBGGqcOTT9ugFPbknYjDj1V0b41jTBqDX0LTR-6NRjYBtdt69AZZYB8LJKab4lHuVPglzwpNZs8Krb3Us6s2g-Fs8L5uq6G8nm8o8J5E2baqQ4iZXXMCueT3UtCqNfkth8qVbL-vCJzzP2gDOwPF_mPLv_68bT4GwAA__-cI3Sf). Miami’s ranking improved two spots to secure the final at-large bid at No. 10, [jumping Notre Dame](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL_unDAQhJ8GOp-MMX-ucJGG7pc2JVrsNbeJ8SJ7c4i3jy51utQzGs33_fhav4AS5f07HOgk8prxqglFsKyROXAJWCrtGQMVyLXwxrIabQbVGaXnNQCle7VtcBHRYN-i66Zx6J7d89m3LxfsHPwcMUybBZjAht4Mo-39bHWcN9uS-6x1Rs9dp8deP8Y4bNFqP8Hgt2mwjdUYSLg8fjJleWFMN7-xPDwfbXIvkbM2_bfGLI1Zrut6-K3Wk4vUT6Mxi-eUcEcVmWWDlBqzfCj_kagzwc0xqgL5F-W9KuFTmUFVTOiFOKv6Owe4Fb-IVRUQVDty2QnUQXCQyiwFVYADG7O0xYXzopSojlNj9X4Apb-_5b9kv535EwAA__-O55hw), which they defeated in their first game of the season. Notre Dame was ahead of Miami in every CFP ranking but Sunday’s final one. Alabama retained its No. 9 ranking after a blowout loss to Georgia in the SEC championship game, setting up a rematch with No. 8 Oklahoma, which had beaten them 23-21 in November. Tulane, set for a rematch with No. 6 Ole Miss, and James Madison, matched up with No. 5 Oregon, rounded out the final two spots, marking the first time multiple [Group of 5](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OqzAQhZ_GdI78i6FwcRu63HZLZPCYzGrCIGMlytuv2FfY-kjnnO_7us_3hIT79j89IbbC8w7vk6A1qHNhzlwz1BO3HTLWtJ-VF26zUcZLbaQa5pyQPrPrciwABmwHUYfe61GPo-0eMSS3eDDWgrY2OxtCtjoHGGEcxl4NHcarTRs1aK16q2598Utxag3Jr0vwTjgFGRvX2zfj3h5Q6MMvqLeVnx3FR2vHKew_YSZhpnRcAFckzET4AmGmxoc0Xq5MBBvIwtyWRCQPJroogrwedDXm441EePZBOLU9E9LvRvuTmFc0PwEAAP__OnZ17A) conference teams have made the playoffs.
## Pearl Harbor Voices Fading
For the first time, Sunday’s Pearl Harbor remembrance ceremony in Hawaii was [held without any survivors present](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u3CAUhJ_G7rziz7AUFGm2u2lTWgc4rIkwWAfiq337yHmFlKORRvN9v762L8gl1_dPONCN1LaK373gGEhbai02ikg9vyvGTFA7Nd_GJphYFy4W9twi5PLZ1BxdQhQoZ3Tc6JVbbq2cdxcsaLNaSNquTFkZvVcQODM6JSm8n7O717hgT86Zluyh0-qTYsHAGrxZ1aQYxjwaPX63XMeOqXzahfQI7ZiL28c4-yR_TOI1iRecN8Bd3YFGDgUn8ToRqCw7kG-0EB54eIIacOl_6MpXo75ArflC6kCfRUJ6iqCTEWCNVVwkY0KSnAmp2RPMTC6e37mU3LWZFHsfkMu_P-O_JF5O_A0AAP__LXWGeA), as only 12 remain alive, all centenarians, and none were able to travel. The [Pearl Harbor National Memorial](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzGOhCAUgOHTQDeGh6BSUGxjN9tuSVAeygbBANHM7TezV5j-z5f8P0_ztCGGtH3bA3Xz2SS8a8TWsBifs8vFYalhS-hCsamWvORmOOPyAfzBJuNsiC8jqNMekWNPUcM4SFCgVE937ezEwVscFKx8lGoRwEAqJ50ExqyiQb814GwCYEPPusHLxQu2jlauyygFEQxdaLl0vzmktqOPr3xh6dZ80Kj31s5K-i_CZ8Ln-767E22Juy1LLnhhavVdEj7Tot15hxhDHUYi2HbYEP-V9tH6pflfAAAA___mCW2k) (run by the National Park Service) notes that the day of remembrance continues every December 7, honoring the 2,403 Americans killed in the 1941 attack. Among the most recent to pass: [Warren "Red" Upton](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD3SnSAUQFeDHQ4gP76CIo3dlzalg3LVm0FwLnw6L6vPvGwhGzjnzPn1NX8FTJj3n-EE37YyZ3hqgtaA5q2UWCgCVdwzRKSQK5WltFkJZbhUXIxzDJjes-6i3wAUDB146ayRL_l6Dd3hnRY2qmFbjJNC29UpOdhxDU6P0qzCdug_NKnEKKWwg-jtZpZNi9UFsy7OaKYFRGyF-t8FcztgS-9yA_VrObvkj9auyoYfTE1MTc_z9N81Y19oZ2o6wx7-YIbK1JTDHRI_sLZCb6amj5apKVyEiamppAi18QsCJX4EWgrx-k033oV4RKg87MClMB35eD2YElbrmBb7GTD9i2n_dfD26m8AAAD__3ZlhUE), the last known survivor who served aboard the _USS Utah_ , died in December 2024 at age 105. Another veteran, Jessie A. Mahaffey, who survived the capsizing of the _USS Oklahoma_ , died in March 2025, at age 102. To ensure survivors’ voices aren’t lost, federal and public agencies have archived hundreds of oral histories, letters, photos, and diaries. Both the National Park Service and the Library of Congress (via its Veterans History Project) maintain extensive collections accessible to the public. Watch [survivor Jessie Mahaffey discuss his memories](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOtCAUBeCnwU4DFwUsKP7Gbv52S4NyGZlF7wTIGN9-477CNqc5yXeS8_WYHy6meDz_ux1tDTQfeJaEtWKeA5Gn7DGX-DzQx-yOkmmhOgOHoRXQcjN7F9M19423ARFQNmiFVoMYxTjKZrNmHYxY9GhwNFpLOS4geqMRF625UmsT7a0J4EYIriTvVBiW0PNVu2Fd9NCznqOPlXL3onjUDUO66IO5W2lvkt1qfRcm_zGYGEzneXbf77TWz10zmG6cwcTlHYrBtNGOlc6j3TBT-8JSIra721wIeDGYmmz9-4wpxaI06_lzdzH9btU_HfSx8BMAAP__f8Z4Tw).

On this day in 1980, The Beatles' superstar was shot and killed by Mark David Chapman as he was entering his Manhattan apartment building, generating worldwide mourning. The tallest Christmas tree in one English town isn’t covered in tinsel or ornaments—it’s made . The 25-foot display, assembled by 20 volunteers over five days, is now drawing crowds and raising money for charity. What's the most unusual Christmas tree you've ever seen? Reply to this email to let us know. Mistake tracking: incorrectly stated that Kentucky became a state in 1891. In fact, Kentucky achieved statehood in 1792; the 1891 date refers to the adoption of a revised state constitution. We regret the error. If you want to give someone on the streets more than just warmth this Christmas, your support of today’s sponsor, , can help turn a night of survival into a moment of real hope with a Homeless Care Kit. | Federal Task Forces to Probe Food Pricing President Trump signed an to crack down on suspected price fixing and anti-competitive practices in the food industry. The Justice Department and Federal Trade Commission will investigate whether big players, including foreign-owned companies, are or threatening the security of the nation’s food supply. If they find wrongdoing, the agencies can bring cases and recommend new rules. Lawmakers will receive progress reports within six months and again at the one-year mark, setting up a comprehensive review of America’s food system. . Miami, Alabama Make College Football Playoff The 12-team College Football Playoff bracket is set, with Indiana earning the No. 1 seed after a 13-0 campaign, followed by Ohio State, Georgia, and Texas Tech, all of which receive first-round byes. . Miami’s ranking improved two spots to secure the final at-large bid at No. 10, , which they defeated in their first game of the season. Notre Dame was ahead of Miami in every CFP ranking but Sunday’s final one. Alabama retained its No. 9 ranking after a blowout loss to Georgia in the SEC championship game, setting up a rematch with No. 8 Oklahoma, which had beaten them 23-21 in November. Tulane, set for a rematch with No. 6 Ole Miss, and James Madison, matched up with No. 5 Oregon, rounded out the final two spots, marking the first time multiple conference teams have made the playoffs. Pearl Harbor Voices Fading For the first time, Sunday’s Pearl Harbor remembrance ceremony in Hawaii was , as only 12 remain alive, all centenarians, and none were able to travel. The (run by the National Park Service) notes that the day of remembrance continues every December 7, honoring the 2,403 Americans killed in the 1941 attack. Among the most recent to pass: , the last known survivor who served aboard the USS Utah , died in December 2024 at age 105. Another veteran, Jessie A. Mahaffey, who survived the capsizing of the USS Oklahoma , died in March 2025, at age 102. To ensure survivors’ voices aren’t lost, federal and public agencies have archived hundreds of oral histories, letters, photos, and diaries. Both the National Park Service and the Library of Congress (via its Veterans History Project) maintain extensive collections accessible to the public. Watch . # [The Assembly Line Is Already Dead -- We're Just Afraid to Admit It](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3159258&post_id=180266653&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMjY2NjUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUxOTU4OTYsImV4cCI6MTc2Nzc4Nzg5NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMxNTkyNTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.bm1p6DyyIPAe-_1tN6LllYX3oObEmEih_Jtu4-8epYw)
### If a robot can do the job while you scroll Instagram, the fight should be for people, not tasks.
| | [The Angry Democrat](https://substack.com/@theangrydem)
---
| Dec 8
---
| [](https://substack.com/@theangrydem)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3159258&post_id=180266653&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMjY2NjUzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NTE5NTg5NiwiZXhwIjoxNzY3Nzg3ODk2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzE1OTI1OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.UEbHKIRZv19v5VwA0CovFlIslHkCcI-mM_0NIHASLTI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mattdiemer/p/workers-deserve-purpose?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

This is going to be an unpopular opinion. And to be fair, I have not fully committed to this idea yet. But it has been stuck in my head since the summer after visiting the Ford F-150 plant in Dearborn, Michigan. What I saw there forced me to confront something I think a lot of people, especially Democrats, are afraid to talk about honestly.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b8cd8e22-5855-40a5-a868-5a43c8d6fdb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Detroit Industry, North Wall, 1932-33_ : _Diego Rivera_
We always hear politicians talk about the dignity of work. Sherrod Brown talks about it all the time. And the dignity of work is real. But job protectionism, the kind that keeps certain jobs alive long after technology can replace them, is not dignity. It actually strips dignity away. It creates unrealistic expectations. And it forces workers to fight a losing battle against the very future that is already here.
Now, that is where people flip out and think I hate workers or manufacturing or the American labor movement. That is not true at all. In fact, this entire idea only exists because I care deeply about workers and their future. So let me explain what I saw.
* * *
**What I Saw in Dearborn Changed Everything**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ad40c3b1-9934-4d53-800c-586ddd03b6a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Detroit Industry Mural : Diego Rivera : 1932-33
At the Ford plant, I watched a man on the assembly line installing some sort of power converter in the back of the F-150. It was repetitive. It was slow. And it was obvious that a machine could do it faster, with fewer errors, and without taking breaks. Between installing each part, the guy would check his phone. Scroll. Look at Instagram. AirPods in, listening to music, responding to messages, then casually going back to the task.
Two things hit me immediately. First, I could not believe that phones were allowed on the floor. Second, I realized why that job still existed. It existed because the union negotiated to keep it from being automated. Not because the job, I assume, required human talent. Not because it required craftsmanship. But because union negotiations protected certain tasks from being handed over to automation.
And that is when I asked myself the real question.
Can you have dignity in work if the only reason your job exists is because a machine is being forced and told to stand down?
If you know a robot hanging over your head can do everything you do, faster, better, more consistently, what does that do to your sense of pride? Your sense of purpose? Your dignity?
We do not like to think about that. But it is happening already.
* * *
**Denial Is The Enemy.**
Jobs have dignity when they require skill. When they require talent. When people walk into a plant and know that only Mike or Sam can perform a certain task with precision. When a new hire takes months or years to get good enough to match them.
That was dignity.
But when a job becomes something a robot could master and the only reason humans still do it is because the contract said so, that pride disappears. It transforms into something else. Something uncomfortable. Something we do not want to admit.
There are factories in the world right now called dark factories. They are literally dark because no humans work inside them. No lighting required. No heating. No safety routines. No breaks. No sick days. No HR mediations. Machines build products twenty four hours a day, seven days a week.
And those factories will produce goods faster, cheaper, and more consistently than any fully human assembly line.
That is the future. Like it or not.
* * *
**Protectionism Does Not Equal Dignity**
Protecting a job does not create pride. It does not create dignity. It does not give a worker a sense of accomplishment. What gives dignity in work is knowing that you are needed. That your skill matters. That your craft exists because you can do something uniquely valuable.
Mere job security is not dignity.
Protection from automation is not dignity.
It is simply delaying the inevitable.
This is not an attack on unions. Unions are vital. They have saved lives. They have raised wages. They have fought for workers' rights. But when negotiations become solely about preventing innovation instead of preparing workers for the next wave of work, that is not protecting the future.
* * *
**The Real Question Is Not How We Protect Jobs. It Is How We Protect People.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3197fe53-f2e9-4d63-a823-1d5d21d9984b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Diego portrayed the Ford stamping press as Coatlicue, the Aztec goddess of life and death, to reflect the machine's history of worker injuries and the goddess's association with human sacrifice.
This is where we need to shift the conversation.
The future is automation. Every country knows it. Every company knows it. Every economist knows it. The question is how society adapts.
Do we allow automation to lower the cost of goods so significantly that abundance becomes normal? Do we use technological progress to give people lives with more time, more freedom, and more opportunity to pursue what genuinely gives them pride?
Or do we keep fighting a losing battle to protect tasks instead of uplifting people?
Because at the end of the day, what gives people pride and dignity is not screwing a part onto a truck all day while checking Instagram.
It is being valuable.
It is being needed.
It is doing something only they can do.
It is having purpose.
And when automation makes certain jobs obsolete, that purpose cannot come from fighting robots. It has to come from building a society where people have the freedom to pursue their real talents, ambitions, and contributions.
p.s. Watch this now 👇🏽

[](https://substack.com/redirect/21029489-a914-4dee-bdd7-360c5379fbdc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**A Hard Truth We Cannot Ignore**
Dignity does not come from being protected from change.
Dignity comes from having a meaningful place in a changing world.
And if we do not face this honestly, we will lose more than jobs. We will lose purpose. We will lose unity. We will lose the chance to build a society where humans do what humans are meant to do.
This is not about killing jobs. It is about redefining dignity.
It is about asking better questions. And it is about being brave enough to imagine a future where work evolves, but people do not get left behind.
Controversial or not, this conversation is coming. I am just choosing to have it before the robots force us to.
* * *
Please share if you are an **Angry Democrat**.

Keep and eye out for my thoughts on UBI and The Union Method

clean_body: If a robot can do the job while you scroll Instagram, the fight should be for people, not tasks. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? We need to find other ANGRY DEMOCRATS. Please consider sharing and subscribing to fuel the ANGER for change. If a robot can do the job while you scroll Instagram, the fight should be for people, not tasks. | | | Dec 8 | | | | | | | | | | | | This is going to be an unpopular opinion. And to be fair, I have not fully committed to this idea yet. But it has been stuck in my head since the summer after visiting the Ford F-150 plant in Dearborn, Michigan. What I saw there forced me to confront something I think a lot of people, especially Democrats, are afraid to talk about honestly. | | ---|---|--- Detroit Industry, North Wall, 1932-33 : Diego Rivera We always hear politicians talk about the dignity of work. Sherrod Brown talks about it all the time. And the dignity of work is real. But job protectionism, the kind that keeps certain jobs alive long after technology can replace them, is not dignity. It actually strips dignity away. It creates unrealistic expectations. And it forces workers to fight a losing battle against the very future that is already here. Now, that is where people flip out and think I hate workers or manufacturing or the American labor movement. That is not true at all. In fact, this entire idea only exists because I care deeply about workers and their future. So let me explain what I saw. What I Saw in Dearborn Changed Everything | | ---|---|--- Detroit Industry Mural : Diego Rivera : 1932-33 At the Ford plant, I watched a man on the assembly line installing some sort of power converter in the back of the F-150. It was repetitive. It was slow. And it was obvious that a machine could do it faster, with fewer errors, and without taking breaks. Between installing each part, the guy would check his phone. Scroll. Look at Instagram. AirPods in, listening to music, responding to messages, then casually going back to the task. Two things hit me immediately. First, I could not believe that phones were allowed on the floor. Second, I realized why that job still existed. It existed because the union negotiated to keep it from being automated. Not because the job, I assume, required human talent. Not because it required craftsmanship. But because union negotiations protected certain tasks from being handed over to automation. And that is when I asked myself the real question. Can you have dignity in work if the only reason your job exists is because a machine is being forced and told to stand down? If you know a robot hanging over your head can do everything you do, faster, better, more consistently, what does that do to your sense of pride? Your sense of purpose? Your dignity? We do not like to think about that. But it is happening already. Denial Is The Enemy. Jobs have dignity when they require skill. When they require talent. When people walk into a plant and know that only Mike or Sam can perform a certain task with precision. When a new hire takes months or years to get good enough to match them. That was dignity. But when a job becomes something a robot could master and the only reason humans still do it is because the contract said so, that pride disappears. It transforms into something else. Something uncomfortable. Something we do not want to admit. There are factories in the world right now called dark factories. They are literally dark because no humans work inside them. No lighting required. No heating. No safety routines. No breaks. No sick days. No HR mediations. Machines build products twenty four hours a day, seven days a week. And those factories will produce goods faster, cheaper, and more consistently than any fully human assembly line. That is the future. Like it or not. Protectionism Does Not Equal Dignity Protecting a job does not create pride. It does not create dignity. It does not give a worker a sense of accomplishment. What gives dignity in work is knowing that you are needed. That your skill matters. That your craft exists because you can do something uniquely valuable. Mere job security is not dignity. Protection from automation is not dignity. It is simply delaying the inevitable. This is not an attack on unions. Unions are vital. They have saved lives. They have raised wages. They have fought for workers' rights. But when negotiations become solely about preventing innovation instead of preparing workers for the next wave of work, that is not protecting the future. The Real Question Is Not How We Protect Jobs. It Is How We Protect People. | | ---|---|--- Diego portrayed the Ford stamping press as Coatlicue, the Aztec goddess of life and death, to reflect the machine's history of worker injuries and the goddess's association with human sacrifice. This is where we need to shift the conversation. The future is automation. Every country knows it. Every company knows it. Every economist knows it. The question is how society adapts. Do we allow automation to lower the cost of goods so significantly that abundance becomes normal? Do we use technological progress to give people lives with more time, more freedom, and more opportunity to pursue what genuinely gives them pride? Or do we keep fighting a losing battle to protect tasks instead of uplifting people? Because at the end of the day, what gives people pride and dignity is not screwing a part onto a truck all day while checking Instagram. It is being valuable. It is being needed. It is doing something only they can do. It is having purpose. And when automation makes certain jobs obsolete, that purpose cannot come from fighting robots. It has to come from building a society where people have the freedom to pursue their real talents, ambitions, and contributions. p.s. Watch this now 👇🏽 A Hard Truth We Cannot Ignore Dignity does not come from being protected from change. Dignity comes from having a meaningful place in a changing world. And if we do not face this honestly, we will lose more than jobs. We will lose purpose. We will lose unity. We will lose the chance to build a society where humans do what humans are meant to do. This is not about killing jobs. It is about redefining dignity. It is about asking better questions. And it is about being brave enough to imagine a future where work evolves, but people do not get left behind. Controversial or not, this conversation is coming. I am just choosing to have it before the robots force us to. Please share if you are an Angry Democrat. Keep and eye out for my thoughts on UBI and The Union Method Join otherANGRY DEMOCRATS** by support Matt's work. Subscribe and Share! | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Matt Diemer 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

# Monday, December 8, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1914, the Southwest Intercollegiate Athletic Conference (later the [Southwest Conference](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1ywyAQQOHToM4aWP3hgiKNOqdNqUGwSDtB4IG1Zd8-k1whF3hv5vu6LTdLkdL2aQ80_Ap5SXjWiMxYFryTc8TvaM_6ID5srfTErdACEoaLgovUi7cU30vfeLNKf1UNGjWNg7oOoMdmN52WfQBwWg8OphG91vYaQDpcR6f60JD5jSmQWoFU0LfeB9ChX6dBddM6daKX6IlzaXlHxpetIb7zE0vr8tFEszPfq-g-BMwC5vM8W667zSlSwjaXTcC82-TXnL8FzJi4EFYBc80P3k-sfHE5BSyYHDbF-PtJMVIdJ9HL7bAU_z78P52ngZ8AAAD__9Fae7k)) was established by eight schools: Texas, Texas A&M, Baylor, Arkansas, Oklahoma, Oklahoma A&M (now Oklahoma State), Southwestern University, and Rice. A webcam installed at the Matagorda County Birding Nature Center offers a 24/7 view of a pair of eagles that have nested there for at least seven years. Two eggs were laid on Thanksgiving. Check it out [here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL8GOpC9MRgKF9eky7VXImMvsJLByN6E8PcnovuD655mNKPR_DyGh6VA2_xtVzT8nuKw4ZEDMmMacCfniM9gj_wkXm3O9MI50QACmkpCJbrBWwrnoEpvRuF7WaKRum1k30DXlotRspM3PerOO9X1IJweJ2cbCQr7XrdNSeYqkyA6CUKCqr2foJvUqJtP8FYogZ44ppoXZHzbPIUzvjDVLq5lMAvznovbVwH3Au7HcdRuSXG73ALugSa8ZAr-D21icuGi1bKdY_K2cvG58VmhnQNWG2aunF0rkABN2-p6X_YyGb8fFALlVhdKzKul8FnA__vtZeA3AAD__yqggjI). Want a smarter way to stay ahead of the markets in 2025? Today’s sponsor, [Anchor Point](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O4yAURp8Gd4n4s80UFCtF2Wq22WJLC8PFuRIYC66TyduvnM0-wbTnOxyJ--dz-nSYcF1-uQyWvmKZVni0BERQJ9jQe6Rnco-2I2XXGt5hqThJLvuTkCdupuAwPSfdBTvz8CE6sGIcevHRSzN0N6ujGXR0sw6jEXGOpudC6HkeHPgYwHRoj5iQ3AjJhdTnEKI0Uc9jL9Q4j4ppDgGp1DPdgODLtZie5Q717Evukr0RbY2pH0xembw28HuFs1v9rW4FVzqsg2O6Qz1V2EqlE2SH6XigrjvlqZW9emDq8i4zORw4Q8A9M3V56y_oy0qwElOXf8llxwD_N5c3h8vK1OX3a2Syl_znYXTVhu2BKWEbRqb5ciRfP6Dv3f1u5d8AAAD__3Kpm-M), says the real story isn't gold, it's the silent surge building in silver.

## Paxton Sues to Stop EPIC City
Attorney General Ken Paxton is suing to [stop the development](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL_uozAQhJ8GOkfYgZgULq5Jl2uvRIu9Tla3GORd8uftT9wr_NqZ0afR9-c-3YGYyuM3LBj0k9ep4FsYVbFOuFGMpF-Gt-ykC4jQCx-VJte5wVhnunFKQPyd-jaFuUtX22Kw_jLY6-DGS_sM0ft8Qex86odrnIc-45jdmK9-hjiO0FI4YNZ1o3Wddf0ppaPvZz_Ys5_9uek7TKRrPekTFT8gmb_rC-sprkvL4am6SXP-1bhb424JmEHws1UUORaNu4mCYuNuG3x0LUZ2FIMgajaGshoShoWiiVgUq4kMtIjJFfa0MxY1eS-pAgmVh4GSzLbGv6iGqRyJxCcuB76tIW1vYia5-KbvHgsQ_z-pP1P7Cu5fAAAA__9B1JNL) of a proposed Muslim-centric community in Collin and Hunt counties, alleging a local mosque and an affiliated real estate development group violated Texas securities laws to fund the 1,000-home EPIC City, which recently rebranded as The Meadow. According to the lawsuit, the East Plano Islamic Center and Community Capital Partners [misrepresented the nature](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0cHOpCAQBOCn0RsTQATn4GGTjbd_r3s0rd04nR_EADPOvP3G_xX2XKlKvtTfr_kLOPC-_YFIY337NO90lkC1Up7p4HXl-glwlifXCKXwi7bMs5a6F0oLOcwIHD6zaXFcJN5VS6Nytlf3Xg-2fYy9RSmXBdVq1J2MJetWp2CQ0IHr_NDyeI0pLQelpdLmhuj14M3ietW5xXWNkYRcU77VB1V6Q_Hhk16Ub2uKbRgftR6l6X41emr0dJ7nzaf3pbjyRk9HClx5LY2eftoCNnHAu6ZdlCcVcSnFxRRILwrpoFwE-EpZHDktJDzvWASEQBuh8BmeKCKXQIC8byI-S-Ao0h4-IkL-psr71nRTLGn9Zmy633p1vXFoyaC0g3FaWuoVSOq1svdBtnnE4-QQuFjXGLlF4PCjq__3yWvU_wIAAP__zeejjg) of the community, failed to secure enough "accredited" investors, and fraudulently enriched themselves using solicited funds. "_The leaders behind EPIC City have engaged in a radical plot to destroy hundreds of acres of beautiful Texas land and line their own pockets_ ," [said Paxton](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuunjAQhJ_GdP5lLxdD4SIN3UmbEtl4AUfLRfYCh7ePOK-Qekaf5ps_X8OXixS3-bdb0fL3tA8b3pmQGdOARxzHyA-5O5-RV5dzvHBOcQAFtdQgVTsEF-kZqiJYr0KnC7TaNLXuamibYrF1qY3pmtH7Snd1i8Yr5b12BtGUYYIi2hemQbUalIbqE8IE7VR5U-vSeFOKSmGIvKcPL8j47fJEz35h-oz7WpBdmI8syl8CegH93zMzL_havLmAfn6724obC-jH_UycJblbQP8Dk455Txs-csYNkyOZT8zydZevvAx4Ie0HpixfknREOGOgR07JneEk3LhINhx3JIq5MaJS8-oi_ezj_3v1svAvAAD__9iCkYg), adding that "_those responsible will be barred from ever creating another fraudulent operation like this again_." Since March, the proposed development has been the subject of multiple state and federal investigations, with at least four ongoing. In June, the Department of Justice [closed a civil rights investigation](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ8GOiN7-C9cbJMu226JHDyG2WswsicxefsrovsGt5opjj6d7_y7T3dDnvblr9lQ8-nCtGNOHpkxTnjQPBO_vcnpSbyZlOiFS6QJJLRCgZDDZA3599SUVj-kHVWJWvVdq8YWhq5cdedQjQg9YFtjM0iwxjg5uHGc5QjGlKQvmAI5KJAKmspaB4NrHn2r6v7R10Uj0RKHWPGKjKdJzr_DC2M1h630emU-UlH_KeBWwC3nXLlwNpfGFSjgdr3XCZFX8QGI7Zk8bWINz0T7Io4Y_uPMYjY8r5jEjln8SAsXwybMIr5wF4c5Oexl1PbI5D2lri8auWyG_KcM_27Cl4bvAAAA__8YOonV) requested by U.S. Sen. John Cornyn, and in September, the Texas Workforce Commission settled a Fair Housing Act complaint.
## Dallas Christmas Display Ignites Debate
The homeowners of a Dallas mansion that went viral last year for its extravagant Christmas display are [doubling down](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuOhDAQRE8DGchuPsaBg03IZtMNUWM3Q0vmI9szHm6_Yq-wWUklVem9n8f0QPa8P79xI5M-yzHtlKOnlChMdLK1nC6POb44bRgjv-kZeAIBXSWhEsPkkP01taUzs3BalmSk6jupOxj6cjXdMDuhWo0KtcBZ9-2CWirQiuzcaFmyucckiEGCkNDWzi0wLO2sOtmoWTVFK8hxOkKdVkr0wbj463hTqO2xld6sKZ2xaL4KGAsYc861XcOx320Bo3359ApUwIghsfV3cug9xmo9NjryTiFWdg0cb8DKcTw9XhVIADH0uj7XswzGnZm959irohXPDdn_vaf_OXsb-A0AAP__BZiCGg) this holiday season with a Grinch theme, turning their front yard into an inflatable-filled Whoville. De Vitis reportedly handed out $4,500 worth of candy and told visitors to "be nice to my neighbors, even though they’re not nice to me." One recent visitor [praised the couple](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUDtuhDAQPY3pQPYAxlu4SEO3aVMi4xlgJAMr7KyX20dwhbTvq_d-nsPTceBt_nYr2fSZ9mGjHAOlRMdAL_ae0xlcjr-cVhcjv2k-eAAJbamglGZAx-EcmgLtKPGhCrKq0616tGB0sdhRm7oFemjV1a6tvZeIExqaNKjGaFOwvcIUSKNAKmgqxAnM1Ixdq-pu7GrRSEJO-1GlhRJ9XJzCub_pqPy-FsEuKb2iqL8E9AL6nHPlx3ituHgB_W0R0F-QgH4-ePNLmRZaCctlD4zuLFe3Rd63El0It7o4LL4yh8BRd6KR8-o43I3pfz-9LfwFAAD__yNUfj4) for "getting you in the spirit," but a neighbor's father, who visits frequently, called the massive crowds a public safety concern. ## Tom Hicks Dies at 79
Tom Hicks, the former owner of the Dallas Stars and the Texas Rangers, [died Saturday](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHSpCAQBOCnwUwLRgENCC4x20svtFCGdepwtWDcXd_-ynuFP-_qrq__PKaHp0Sv52-_oeNv3KcXfkpCZswTHrQsxFfyn3ISb74UeuMz0wQSdK2glv0UPKVr6qrgZhkGVaFT1mg1aOhNtTqtrIlKooceDcSgdFjaAczQWd1Foytyd5kC2SuQCromhAh97GarVWtn24pOYiDec8MrMn59iena35ibZd-q5Fbmo4j2l4BRwBh8Sr40y5n4zLj5404JGG-WgPHm1IkiChh53-qVlr-l3mfi0-dLwFhlF44PpUTFWNHJ5-Yp_V_in_3zdvAvAAD__9FEes0) at the age of 79. He is remembered as an "eternal optimist" who "dreamed big" and "a visionary that forever changed the Dallas/Fort Worth sports landscape." After amassing a fortune as a private equity manager, Hicks purchased the Stars in 1995 and the Rangers in 1998. In 1999, the Stars [won the Stanley Cup](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuhSAUxvGnwQ0jRxAdGLqw3a4dDcpRT4pigF6vb9_cvkLnX_Lly__rMT4cBTrWT7ejKa8ljgdeOWApmEY8aZ6p3MFd-YfK7nKmJ66JRmhAcQG86UfvKNyjrLyZGj-ICo3QnRKDgr6rNuNdp6RSqOQ8ox-g7aZhmtH7BQQitBWZ95iAphfQCJD1m_pFTlqJVk-6ZbJBTyWmumxY8OXyEu74xFTPca-C2Uo5M2s_GFgG9rquGvN5vJGB3cPEwOYS083AjgwseQZWamjFoDsGtsSdbzR_Z77EtGPi8Tow8eSOFVPmubiUuSfMXA9VMv68KATKnWayWXdH4e9G-V-8p4HfAAAA__84tIQb), and in 2000, Hicks signed slugger Alex Rodriguez to a record-breaking 10-year, $252 million deal. Hicks was instrumental in [building the American Airlines Center](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O5CAQRk-DM1pQ_NkBwSbOZtMNrbIpukuL2xaw7enbr2auMPEnfe_p_flYPpALP--_cafYP_OxPOlqhXqnutDJ28b9XfBq_7jv2Bq_6F55AQVOapBqXBJyeS92SHFVadIDRR2805OD0Q-P6GEKY1rRTWrUWrnNTN5ml41FD5TswPHrTIMaNSgN9pZShjHbNThtwhqMsIoS96Pe-oM6fWLL5X28qN62Yx9KfPR-NmF-CZgFzNd13a6M-DUKmLF23goJmNt51N4EzP3Y5YO3v00mpibDJCs-71SbbB1rkwlLwSa_SQJmGINMqwlqWpUMPmlpwZBENEr6vIHRAV0mM9SYzotL4eaDsOq-I5dvxf6zsK8I_wMAAP__TkeK0w) in Dallas, establishing the Frisco RoughRiders as a Double-A affiliate of the Rangers, and advocating for the Rangers and Houston Astros to face each other annually before they became division rivals. Hicks also owned 50% of Liverpool FC in the English Premier League. Bankruptcy forced Hicks to sell his sports assets in 2010 and 2011. He is survived by his wife of 35 years, Cinda Cree Hicks, as well as six children and 14 grandchildren.

Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Paxton Sues to Stop EPIC City Attorney General Ken Paxton is suing to of a proposed Muslim-centric community in Collin and Hunt counties, alleging a local mosque and an affiliated real estate development group violated Texas securities laws to fund the 1,000-home EPIC City, which recently rebranded as The Meadow. According to the lawsuit, the East Plano Islamic Center and Community Capital Partners of the community, failed to secure enough "accredited" investors, and fraudulently enriched themselves using solicited funds. "The leaders behind EPIC City have engaged in a radical plot to destroy hundreds of acres of beautiful Texas land and line their own pockets ," , adding that "those responsible will be barred from ever creating another fraudulent operation like this again." Since March, the proposed development has been the subject of multiple state and federal investigations, with at least four ongoing. In June, the Department of Justice requested by U.S. Sen. John Cornyn, and in September, the Texas Workforce Commission settled a Fair Housing Act complaint. Dallas Christmas Display Ignites Debate The homeowners of a Dallas mansion that went viral last year for its extravagant Christmas display are this holiday season with a Grinch theme, turning their front yard into an inflatable-filled Whoville. The city spent $25,375 to in the Preston Hollow neighborhood on eight nights last year, drawing complaints from neighbors who called the home a public nuisance. Homeowner Ryan De Vitis was cited for noise and light glare, prompting him to call his neighbors "the real-life Grinch." The city in October, this time for obstructing visibility with its Halloween display. De Vitis reportedly handed out $4,500 worth of candy and told visitors to "be nice to my neighbors, even though they’re not nice to me." One recent visitor for "getting you in the spirit," but a neighbor's father, who visits frequently, called the massive crowds a public safety concern. "Every night I am here, I worry about an ambulance coming to pick up a kid ," . Tom Hicks Dies at 79 Tom Hicks, the former owner of the Dallas Stars and the Texas Rangers, at the age of 79. He is remembered as an "eternal optimist" who "dreamed big" and "a visionary that forever changed the Dallas/Fort Worth sports landscape." After amassing a fortune as a private equity manager, Hicks purchased the Stars in 1995 and the Rangers in 1998. In 1999, the Stars , and in 2000, Hicks signed slugger Alex Rodriguez to a record-breaking 10-year, $252 million deal. Hicks was instrumental in in Dallas, establishing the Frisco RoughRiders as a Double-A affiliate of the Rangers, and advocating for the Rangers and Houston Astros to face each other annually before they became division rivals. Hicks also owned 50% of Liverpool FC in the English Premier League. Bankruptcy forced Hicks to sell his sports assets in 2010 and 2011. He is survived by his wife of 35 years, Cinda Cree Hicks, as well as six children and 14 grandchildren. | Flying together with our sponsor Gold has been stealing the headlines all year... but the quiet story almost no one is talking about is silver. The price of silver quietly doubled in 2025 —and the reason has nothing to do with Wall Street hype. Silver isn’t just a precious metal. It’s an industrial necessity. Solar panels, EVs, medical tech, electronics—they all consume silver, and once it’s used, it’s gone for good. Supply is falling short, demand keeps climbing, and analysts now believe silver’s supply crunch could create another massive silver boom in 2026. The company has declined to suspend its service when buses are running. () ➤ Laredo: U.S. Border Patrol officials discussed plans for installing a new Smart Wall system along the Rio Grande on Friday. "This idea that it has to be the wall or technology—that's a false narrative ," Chief Patrol Agent Jesse D. Munoz said. "What’s coming is both." () ➤ Celina: Police have called on attorneys representing alleged victims of former Celina ISD coach Caleb Elliott to provide evidence for claims of wrongdoing in four civil lawsuits. () ➤ Starr County: Officials broke ground Tuesday on Texas State Loop 195, which will connect the Rio Grande Valley to Laredo. "We have been waiting a long, long time for this project ," said Starr County Judge Eloy Vera. () ➤ Austin: Homeowners Ryan and Eden O'Malley are preparing for more than a year of construction in their yard for a city drainage project. They won't get any compensation from the city because an easement was sold for $1 in 1958. name: Trump slipped in, Jacob Davis | FIU
subject: Trump’s Buried Clause Now Pouring Cash ∕ Monday, December 8, 2025
body:
Few today will remember this... But in 2018, Trump was embroiled in a battle for America. | | | [-](https://links.research.firstinvestmentupdates.com/u/click?_t=6c5e8d0087884bb7b7cd2e3abb0e6b5f&_m=9eeb28191678461bbe75db720ce63f39&_e=6h_QAJtbYZbcNe4s6ebrzC-nRsTEbb2WwsuptWqwEFEjPPanQckSRmCV1-wsoQKBrWgazK6U__TNw6l7Np0m-BnfUUgwpyqy7OXAsp-jMghU9I9bwjfzF-opjOAlBo6ulxNK698VIE4gKW1BS0-hL4iMH7E41lgDyPInU7edfEkTIH8fyM8kW3ZbYrvpTtvxCQN9HwQb8Gd3uEjc5topsTwJYaWJxRnllNwmrNC-BZEu5jSyCw9wyFMVibiwOdJEKC86abxsiOEuccnRP4Qu2razTndg6FxOaidq8KtvhBOV9LnItPvsUa8w_dvSx6FowTN8yVc5LIoIiwbRjptxuAZvpSs5N3tx6ApyDX1S_YNEnQ9hDFDwITbBldTD-g8q)

**Don't want to receive our publications?**[**Unsubscribe here**](https://links.research.firstinvestmentupdates.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=6c5e8d0087884bb7b7cd2e3abb0e6b5f&_s=9eeb28191678461bbe75db720ce63f39&_t=hzVD9NBTgaL7niHLXYR-Vg-T8LyoftM4TbOHOuJnIhPcYxA-hHjI5MreQ1nQmlxTSp4iB0HIzGoXr_Ugic8jNMpYKctrj2DwjOgp6Urds3rxo24ECXc1fEb0gPuazj6D)
---
|
---
| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. What [Melissa Corrigan, she/her](https://open.substack.com/users/13839118-melissa-corrigan-sheher?utm_source=mentions) and [Nick Paro](https://open.substack.com/users/189675044-nick-paro?utm_source=mentions) walked us through is nothing short of a five-alarm fire for anyone who cares about the rule of law, the U.S. military, or basic human decency. This isn't some abstract policy dispute. This is the Department of Defense ordering sailors to **delete evidence** of living, wounded human beings rescued after an unlawful boat strike -- and a group of brave service members refusing, pushing back, and documenting exactly how wrong it was. Hello, **#DeleteGate**.
Melissa and [David Shuster](https://open.substack.com/users/16891317-david-shuster?utm_source=mentions) previously reported on the brewing scandal in this piece:
## [EXCLUSIVE: US Navy Deleted Video of October Boat Attack Survivors](https://substack.com/redirect/5dff0c21-bf9e-409b-8272-8f455fbba432?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Melissa Corrigan, she/her](https://substack.com/profile/13839118-melissa-corrigan-sheher) and [David Shuster](https://substack.com/profile/16891317-david-shuster)| | *
---|---|---
| Dec 6
[](https://substack.com/redirect/5dff0c21-bf9e-409b-8272-8f455fbba432?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[On a crystal blue sea, multiple explosive assaults from the sky have blown small boats to pieces, almost always ending the lives of those on board.](https://substack.com/redirect/5dff0c21-bf9e-409b-8272-8f455fbba432?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/5dff0c21-bf9e-409b-8272-8f455fbba432?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
This story is too important to miss. Please share!
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=180923945&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwOTIzOTQ1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjUxOTk2MzUsImV4cCI6MTc2Nzc5MTYzNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.w-ZD6YbZYZkwS1uoamy8fzFrZVVCA9PZy319JExdACg)
* * *
Melissa starts by laying out how this bombshell landed in her lap: a trusted DOD source sent her a document 46 days prior, describing what happened aboard the USS _Iwo Jima_ on October 17. And from the moment she saw it, the veteran in her kicked in. Orders to delete anything -- especially photos and video of detainees -- are a giant, screaming red klaxon.
She knew instantly this wasn't just a story; this was a moral emergency. Her first priority was protecting the whistleblowers who risked their careers to come forward. Through a quiet chain of veterans, she got the document into the hands of the Senate Armed Services Committee before anyone ever said a word publicly.
That's how you do it when the stakes are this high.
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.3DCADiziGzjEg7UfZCRhLK2aGW1JKnnSoM_gyCtDgeo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=180923945)
* * *
Then Nick -- Army sergeant, trained interrogator, expert on detainee procedures -- breaks down just how profoundly illegal all of this is. The United States is **not** in a congressionally authorized armed conflict with Venezuela. No war declared, no authorization, no oversight, no nothing.
Which means every person seized at sea is legally a **civilian** until proven otherwise, not some magically self-justifying "narco-terrorist." And the law -- U.S. law, military law, international law -- could not be clearer: you document everything. You collect biometrics. You record medical status. You keep chain-of-custody logs. You do **not** delete evidence. You **do** convene a competent tribunal. None of that happened.
Instead, someone -- almost certainly above the Admiral level -- ordered everything wiped. And the enlisted sailors and Marines on that ship knew immediately it was wrong. They fought it. They went to JAG. And they were overridden.
Nick had previously, graciously, allowed us to republish a story he'd written about "Unprivileged Belligerents":
## [Wounded and Unknown: Treatment and Release Procedures for "Unprivileged Belligerents"](https://substack.com/redirect/d81b805a-05ae-436f-abfd-021b5dabca68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/profile/312330913-blue-amp) and [Blue Amp Media](https://substack.com/profile/50954540-blue-amp-media)| | *
---|---|---
| Dec 6
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d81b805a-05ae-436f-abfd-021b5dabca68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d81b805a-05ae-436f-abfd-021b5dabca68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/d81b805a-05ae-436f-abfd-021b5dabca68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Melissa points out the devastating downstream consequences. One of the detainees was returned to Colombia basically comatose. Another was released by Ecuador because the U.S. had **no** evidence he'd done anything wrong. Think about that: if these men were the "narco-terrorists" the Pentagon claims, they would have been paraded around as proof.
Instead, the Defense Department destroyed the only documentation that could have helped their own argument -- or cleared the innocent. That's not just suspicious; that's catastrophic leadership failure.
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.3DCADiziGzjEg7UfZCRhLK2aGW1JKnnSoM_gyCtDgeo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=180923945)
* * *
They both hammer the heart of the issue: this isn't on the lower-ranked service members. They tried to do everything right. This is a **top-down** moral collapse under Pete Hegseth and whoever in the Trump orbit green-lit these illegal strikes. And let's be clear: if a private citizen killed 85 people with no legal authority, we wouldn't debate terminology -- we'd call it murder.
Melissa and Nick end with a call to action: whistleblowers have legal avenues (like [the ](https://substack.com/redirect/f8a62820-3b9d-4180-8686-26f028f6c415?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[Orders Project](https://substack.com/redirect/f8a62820-3b9d-4180-8686-26f028f6c415?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**), Congress has the responsibility to intervene, and journalists -- especially veteran-journalists -- have the obligation to keep shining light on this. Because this isn't just about foreign policy.
It's about whether the United States still follows its own laws or lets a handful of political arsonists use the military like a video game.
A game we're all losing.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f851ad88-9d5b-4140-9e34-ac22ef610bfe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
| A guest post by| [Nick Paro](https://substack.com/@nickparo?utm_campaign=guest_post_bio&utm_medium=email)Your Army veteran, tech deconstructionist, and new favorite poet! The host of the "Sick of this Show" and co-founder of the "Banner & Backbone" network. Here to reignite civics and redefine masculinity.[Subscribe to Nick](https://substack.com/redirect/c25032d5-0445-4ebb-8763-96a455aef4ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

name: The PreBorn! Team
subject: Your gift saves lives and shares the gospel
body:
For nearly two decades, PreBorn! has been standing in the gap for mothers and babies across the nation.

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z74g43a3hmd4oim3rrdpicamds4g2k9ngt03oj2g_rp22sh2s8i6eor5c9i6ao34c9h2bs)
---
Hi Advocate for Life, For nearly two decades, **PreBorn!** has been standing in the gap for mothers and babies across the nation. We do two simple but powerful and life-changing things: We help women in crisis **see their babies and hear their heartbeats through free ultrasounds** , and we offer **gospel-centered, compassionate care** at our Network Clinics. Those two things change everything and we can’t do them without people like you. When a mother hears her child’s heartbeat for the first time, fear gives way to faith.

A tiny miracle begins the story of a mighty future.

Hope breaks through the darkness. Your **$28 gift provides one life-saving ultrasound** and shares the love of Christ with a woman in need. As our thank-you for your gift, you’ll receive the [**_Tiny Miracles, Mighty Futures 2026 Calendar_**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zdgod9tkr28n9pk72i8ghlbo4b8b25k89kqnhm48_rp22sh2s8i6eor5c9i6ao34c9h2bs) __ filled with **scripture and a monthly prayer guide** to help you lift up mothers, babies, and the ministry of PreBorn! all year long in prayer.
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z4esopmd7uhibe3regpph9bq9723vpptraa09gpo_rp22sh2s8i6eor5c9i6ao34c9h2bs)
---
Calendars are limited during this busy year-end season, so give now to **save a baby, share the gospel, and celebrate the gift of life**.
---
[**Give $28 + Get Your Calendar**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zds0mi3icb6q7a5sqikpp8brupt25mdgn5bqo8hg_rp22sh2s8i6eor5c9i6ao34c9h2bs)
---
We can’t wait to hear from you! Gratefully, The PreBorn! Team
---

name: Sign Your Name | Left Action
subject: Demand FIFA Withdraw its Absurd "Peace Prize" for Trump
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Demand FIFA Withdraw its Absurd "Peace Prize" for Trump](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te2db627c-4676-4693-826c-ac728c3cb2ee/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf750b856-dda7-4078-9104-e9927616e420/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | _(After you take this action, talk about it and see others on on[our new Substack page](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb3e776c2-94d6-448d-b7ae-1ffc39225063/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)! Thanks to the [20,000+](https://default.salsalabs.org/T29c96457-8cda-475c-9f4f-85744502584a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) there!)_ What an embarrassment. FIFA (the governing body for international soccer) just invented a made-up “peace prize” to flatter Donald Trump. In an act both fallacious and fellatious at the same time, FIFA presented the award to Trump onstage, with much fanfare, as if this were a real thing. (I guess they figured they already had plenty of knee pads handy.) [**Take action, and demand FIFA withdraw this ridiculous prize.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T02e42b08-52ad-4353-80c8-c26d1be7cb34/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) This award didn’t exist until FIFA President Gianni Infantino made it up out of the blue, claiming Trump had somehow advanced “peace” through the 2026 World Cup. (which, like Trump’s work for peace, hasn’t actually happened yet). It’s frankly crazy that FIFA would give a peace prize to a guy who likes to bomb new countries because he’s angry he can’t spell them, cheers on war crimes, and who has destructively stupid-walked his way through actual peace negotiations, treaties, and conventions like Godzilla going through Tokyo. It’s also incredibly dangerous. Soccer is wildly popular in nearly every country on Earth, with billions of fans. And this essentially gives a seal of approval from the sport to Trump, something he can leverage for support of even stupider global efforts. [**Take action, and demand FIFA withdraw this ridiculous prize.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5f5d7869-1ab8-42d6-8f1e-a0cd6b09408d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | | [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9eaebda7-3361-4b5b-83ec-874386ef3f7a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---

---
|

It’s also incredibly dangerous. Soccer is wildly popular in nearly every country on Earth, with billions of fans. And this essentially gives a seal of approval from the sport to Trump, something he can leverage for support of even stupider global efforts. | | | | | | | Thank you, Left Action | | | | | | Other things you can do: Follow us on * Follow Us | | LeftAction Washington, District of Columbia info@leftaction.com | Having trouble viewing this email? | | | or

name: Andrew Yang
subject: Netflix Wins the Streaming War
body:
Well, the streaming wars are over, and Netflix won.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..3s2d0bua7pVzHV-CyFohuyq71peWRvsYDnzm8PDXx44?) for more
---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5d055300-3b57-46d2-984c-5376b7040e0d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
# [Netflix Wins the Streaming War](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=181036760&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMDM2NzYwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUxOTk0MzEsImV4cCI6MTc2Nzc5MTQzMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.cAEcBklmVxh-Ioiw_spCSsc7zTz8DLsj52BKiacMgoA)
| | [Andrew Yang](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---
| Dec 8
---
| [](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=181036760&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMDM2NzYwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NTE5OTQzMSwiZXhwIjoxNzY3NzkxNDMxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjg3MzA0Iiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.eZbURye9_mo0EwmNn62CdH8HAkHUGhHCRHXQOIuNVus&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=181036760&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMDM2NzYwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUxOTk0MzEsImV4cCI6MTc2Nzc5MTQzMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.cAEcBklmVxh-Ioiw_spCSsc7zTz8DLsj52BKiacMgoA&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=181036760&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxMDM2NzYwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjUxOTk0MzEsImV4cCI6MTc2Nzc5MTQzMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.cAEcBklmVxh-Ioiw_spCSsc7zTz8DLsj52BKiacMgoA)
---
| | [](https://substack..ggEN9n50NnRaFUc1oww1xLWOJ_Y73J5oYjia_c4kypU?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/andrewyang/p/netflix-wins-the-streaming-war?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Well, the streaming wars are over, and Netflix won.
The announcement came out on Friday that Netflix would be buying Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and content assets, including HBO Max, for a staggering $83 billion. Netflix beat out Paramount Skydance and Comcast Universal.
Investors are responding as if they expect the merger to be approved by the federal government. Netflix agreed to a $5 billion breakup fee if the merger doesn't go through.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9db99261-67f8-4918-90f0-7ba466b7bbcf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I actually think this deal being approved is very much up in the air. I don't think the Trump crew loves Netflix. This merger could look like an antitrust violation because Netflix and HBO Max are two of the top 5 streaming services in the U.S. and combining them seems somewhat anti-competitive. And consumers understand both of these brands and wouldn't like the idea of them becoming a single entity. Paramount is attempting to keep the bidding war going saying their offer was higher.
The natural expectation is that prices would go up with this merger. $83 billion has got to come from somewhere. I ran a poll on social media: 47% didn't like this merger, 17% liked it, and 36% didn't know.
The streaming wars always seemed like something of a winner-take-all environment, as most people don't have the money, time or appetite to subscribe to Netflix, Amazon Prime, Hulu, Paramount, HBO Max, Disney, Peacock, Apple TV, and ESPN, let alone Starz or AMC. The average household signs up for 4 streaming services. Netflix has scale. Amazon and Apple have infinite money to burn. Disney has decades of legacy content. Everyone else is on the outside looking in.
On a personal note, which streamers do I watch? I did the Disney bundle with Hulu and ESPN. Netflix of course. HBO Max for Game of Thrones and its successors. Prime gets bundled with some other stuff. I sprang for Apple to watch Silo. That puts me at 7, almost twice the national average.
I'm old enough to remember when AOL bought . . . Time Warner, the predecessor company to Warner Bros. Discovery, back in 2000. Back then, there was an investor saying that "Content is king." Today, content is more of a subject to distribution and the algorithm. Netflix can make a massive hit out of a random movie like "Gods of Egypt" or a forgotten show like "Suits" just by putting it front and center in front of everyone. Scale begets scale.
One concern that many have with this merger is that the movie theatre experience will die. Box office receipts and attendance have dropped precipitously as more and more Americans stay home and wait for it to hit their TV. Netflix has famously avoided the multiplex for most of its releases, while Warner Brothers Discovery is trying to drag people back out to the theatre to watch Supergirl. Event movies may also go the way of the algorithm in the new world.
There's something special about a large group of people experiencing a film together in a theatre. Enjoy it while you can.
This week on the podcast I talk to Jono Wilde, the new head of the Good Life Movement about making mental health a public policy priority. Want to look up? Email [matt@noblemobile.com](https://substack.com/redirect/d598ba14-8acc-45ed-b84e-a51501ff06a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) if you want a few months off from your wireless bill and be less screen-consumed with [Noble Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/0064dbb0-5c55-485c-8969-f7c99679ced2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). [Offline](https://substack.com/redirect/92600afa-dc06-45ec-9762-8e31f712d473?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s IRL no-phones party is coming to Baltimore, NYC, and Miami upcoming. My new [book](https://substack.com/redirect/fd0fc82e-b9c4-45d8-b25a-41338c801c72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) comes out in February. Remember to touch grass.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5d055300-3b57-46d2-984c-5376b7040e0d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

| Thank you to the Republican Party of Shelby County's Outreach Co-Chairs Kristin New, Kristen Hicks, Lisa Arnold, and Mitch Arnold for all of their hard work building the Christmas float for the Bartlett parade. Our volunteers hung around in the cold for four hours waiting for the parade to start. But it was all worth it because the Republican Party was received enthusiastically by the Bartlett residents.

As the Republican Party of Shelby County says, “**2026 is coming. It’s time to TAKE BACK Shelby County**!”

name: With Honor Action
subject: A Renewed Fight for Peace in Ukraine 🇺🇦
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
New congressional push to bring a fair and lasting peace in Ukraine. | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/withhonor/invitation-two-parties-two-veterans-one-conversation-rep-lalota-rep-ryan-special-session-with-president-carter-alumni-14055330?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | | [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=02559c4dab&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | | Friends,

With Honor Action is working with our allies in Congress to ensure that any agreement to end the Russian invasion of Ukraine preserves Ukraine’s sovereignty, territorial integrity, and long-term ability to defend itself.

➡️ Rep. Don Bacon, a Founding Co-Chair of the For Country Caucus and U.S. Air Force veteran, [forcefully rejects](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=f7b6c2d7c5&e=33f53a5b1a) any proposal that would pressure Ukraine into yielding to Russia’s demands.

➡️ Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, a Co-Chair of the Ukraine Caucus who served as an FBI agent, moves to [force a vote](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=932563aa6d&e=33f53a5b1a) on the stalled bipartisan Russia sanctions bill.

➡️ A bipartisan bill to [enhance support to identify and recover kidnapped Ukrainian children](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=d930b78a62&e=33f53a5b1a) is on the cusp of being passed in the annual defense policy bill.
---
| [Policy Blog: Why the U.S. Must Act Now to Hold Russia Accountable](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=4c7080ed08&e=33f53a5b1a "Policy Blog: Why the U.S. Must Act Now to Hold Russia Accountable")
---
| This newest round of U.S.-Ukraine-Russia negotiations comes at a pivotal moment, as global leaders, defense officials, and lawmakers gathered at the Halifax International Security Forum, where Ukraine "[dominated the agenda](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=7f6a8f91f0&e=33f53a5b1a)."

That’s why With Honor was proud to serve as an official sponsor of this year’s Halifax Forum, reflecting our commitment to working with our allies in Congress and across democratic nations to ensure any deal to end Russia’s invasion preserves Ukraine’s sovereignty, long-term security, and the democratic values at the heart of this fight.
---
| | "Yes, we are weaker as democracies because we have to find a common will to act, but the only thing we have stronger is that free people fight better." — Lesia Orobets, Founder, Price of Freedom
& Former Member of the Ukrainian Parliament
---
|
---
_With Honor's Vice President of Government Affairs, Sean McClintock, leads a conversation on the global rules-based order._
| In the midst of active negotiations over the future of Ukraine, it’s more important than ever to be in the room with like-minded democracies committed to collective security and the defense of sovereign nations. The bipartisan U.S. Congressional Delegation's attendance sent a strong message and reaffirmed the U.S.'s commitment to our democratic allies and partners. We look forward to carrying that momentum into the months ahead as Congress still must pass the annual National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) and annual appropriations bills.
---
| [Highlights from Halifax 🇨🇦](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=238494a4af&e=33f53a5b1a "Highlights from Halifax 🇨🇦")
---
|
---
_Front row: U.S. Senators Chris Coons, Mike Rounds, and Angus King; Senators Peter Welch and Thom Tillis directly behind them on the right._
| In 2025, With Honor participated in the Munich Security Conference, London Defence Conference, and Halifax International Security Forum. All three of these international security conferences continue to have strong bipartisan support and send a strong message to our adversaries that despite our political differences, the U.S. and our allies and partners are committed to democracy and will fight to preserve our freedoms.

Thank you for standing with us.
---
|
---
_Senator Chris Coons and With Honor's Vice President of Government Affairs Sean McClintock._
|
---

---
| | [](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=fe62da37e9&e=33f53a5b1a)

clean_body: New congressional push to bring a fair and lasting peace in Ukraine. | | | | | | | | | | | | | Friends, With Honor Action is working with our allies in Congress to ensure that any agreement to end the Russian invasion of Ukraine preserves Ukraine’s sovereignty, territorial integrity, and long-term ability to defend itself. ➡️ Rep. Don Bacon, a Founding Co-Chair of the For Country Caucus and U.S. Air Force veteran, any proposal that would pressure Ukraine into yielding to Russia’s demands. ➡️ Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, a Co-Chair of the Ukraine Caucus who served as an FBI agent, moves to on the stalled bipartisan Russia sanctions bill. ➡️ A bipartisan bill to is on the cusp of being passed in the annual defense policy bill. | "Policy Blog: Why the U.S. Must Act Now to Hold Russia Accountable" | This newest round of U.S.-Ukraine-Russia negotiations comes at a pivotal moment, as global leaders, defense officials, and lawmakers gathered at the Halifax International Security Forum, where Ukraine "." That’s why With Honor was proud to serve as an official sponsor of this year’s Halifax Forum, reflecting our commitment to working with our allies in Congress and across democratic nations to ensure any deal to end Russia’s invasion preserves Ukraine’s sovereignty, long-term security, and the democratic values at the heart of this fight. | | "Yes, we are weaker as democracies because we have to find a common will to act, but the only thing we have stronger is that free people fight better." — Lesia Orobets, Founder, Price of Freedom & Former Member of the Ukrainian Parliament | With Honor's Vice President of Government Affairs, Sean McClintock, leads a conversation on the global rules-based order. | In the midst of active negotiations over the future of Ukraine, it’s more important than ever to be in the room with like-minded democracies committed to collective security and the defense of sovereign nations. The bipartisan U.S. Congressional Delegation's attendance sent a strong message and reaffirmed the U.S.'s commitment to our democratic allies and partners. We look forward to carrying that momentum into the months ahead as Congress still must pass the annual National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) and annual appropriations bills. | "Highlights from Halifax 🇨🇦" | Front row: U.S. Senators Chris Coons, Mike Rounds, and Angus King; Senators Peter Welch and Thom Tillis directly behind them on the right. | In 2025, With Honor participated in the Munich Security Conference, London Defence Conference, and Halifax International Security Forum. All three of these international security conferences continue to have strong bipartisan support and send a strong message to our adversaries that despite our political differences, the U.S. and our allies and partners are committed to democracy and will fight to preserve our freedoms. Thank you for standing with us. | Senator Chris Coons and With Honor's Vice President of Government Affairs Sean McClintock. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC. | With Honor Action is a 501(c)(4), which serves as the organization’s policy and social-welfare arm. You can request your ticket for this event by visiting:[ https://sheridan.church ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26614794.13723.1.bceff781024bf6a728a124f51399cae2) **Dates** : June 19-20 Please consider giving to the mission of[Sheridan](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26614794.13724.1.8404d6c1235037f440687170f5c61002): [https://sheridan.church/give/](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=26614794.13725.1.80a0b99248be562fe39f9352e76e331a)
